body:
Dear Team,


Good Morning!!


Just as the cicada's unyielding song pierces through the summer air, our unwavering dedication can cut through life's challenges. Persistence is key to overcoming obstacles and achieving greatness. The cicada's steady cry reminds us to stay focused, work tirelessly, and trust the process. Every effort, no matter how small, contributes to our growth and success. Let perseverance be your guiding force.



Regards,


Team HR

body:
Dear Team,

 

This is to inform you all that those whose gross salary for the Financial Year 2024-2025 was less than Rs.750,000/- their Form 16 has been generated.

 

They can access and download their form 16 for above mentioned period from HRMIS portal.

 

Please follow the steps outlined below to access and download your Form 16:

 

1.	Log in to the HRMIS portal using your individual credentials.

 

2.	Select "Self Service" module.

 

3.	Select "Taxation."

 

4.	Click on "Download Form 16."

 

5.	Choose the relevant financial year and proceed to download the document.

 

Incase of any query please do not hesitate to contact me.

 

Regards,

Supriya K.

Noted your below mail. 

 

I would like to inform you that Mumbai has two ports. One is Mumbai Port where all the break bulk vessels call and Nhava Sheva Port is the port where container vessels call. Nhava Sheva Port has one terminal ( shall water berth) where break bulk vessels call but its very rare. So in your below mail, you have mentioned about Nhava Sheva as POL. body:
Dear Saatvik / sir,

MSC offer as follows


We are pleased to offer $ 1,41,000/- tackle/tackle (Includes freight 1xBBK Units & 04x40’FR , 11x40 HC , 1x20 DV , 1x40’OT), from Nhava sheva to Tincan, approx. 41-45 days T/T AGW WOG WP.  

 

BBK Cargo Details……..

LWH 14.7 x 1.70 x 1.85 Mtr/11Ton/2xBBK

 

Container Cargo Details……                                  

40’FR: THC + LOCALS AT BOTH ENDS WITH STANDARD FREE DAYS BOTH ENDS

40’HC: THC + LOCALS AT BOTH ENDS WITH STANDARD FREE DAYS BOTH ENDS

40’OT: THC + LOCALS AT BOTH ENDS WITH STANDARD FREE DAYS BOTH ENDS

 

 

Terms and Conditions as per below…..

1.	Subject to MSC Booking Note & BL clauses
2.	Subject to ondeck/underdeck carrier’s choice  
3.	Subject to space, stowage, planner approval, MSC vessel service & equipment availability, terminal berthing availability at both ends.
4.	Subject to technical GA diagram/photos and final lifting approvals from terminals and from shipper/booking party.
5.	Offered rates are for Non-Military and Non Haz cargo
6.	Subject to clear lifting point, sub to COG of the cargo and cargo must be free from any obstacle to lift.
7.	Tackle/Tackle terms covers loading (single lift per unit)/discharge (single lift per unit)/freight charges.  Tackle to Tackle terms- direct delivery under the hook by full liability of the Shipper/Booking party/consignee and under direct receiving under the hook by the full liability of the Shipper/Booking party/Consignee, unless local law prohibits.
8.	Shipper/Consignee/Booking Party to ensure all local processes are in order prior to vessel arrival.
9.	Subject to minimum 14 days advance intimation for space & equipment arrangement on each vessel.  
10.	The carrier acts as agent to the merchant/booking party/shipper/consignee in arranging for the stevedores to load and stow the cargo and to discharge the cargo. This operation is for the expense and risk of the merchant/booking party/shipper/consignee. Floating Crane operation always on full liability of the Shipper/Booking party/Consignee. Carrier act only facilitator to appoint floating crane.
11.	Shipper to give BBK cargo direct under hook on wharf OR to give on terminal mafi strictly as per loadport terminal requirement. In case underhook loading from shipper’s vehicle and If shipper fails to place the vehicle on time & if vessel will be on hold, then vessel detention & terminal penalty applicable on shipper/consignee/booking party account. Any indirect delivery charges will be on shipper/booking party/consignee account.
12.	In case loading from barge or delivery to barge, same needs to confirm and intimate us prior rate confirmation and prior loading at load port by booking party; any discrepancy at later stage shall not be entertain.
13.	For BBK cargo Lifting/discharge operation shall be carried with available lifting equipment’s with vessel/stevedoring company. If any special requirement of lifting/discharge equipment’s shall be arranged by shipper/consignee/booking party.  
14.	For BBK cargo with regards to lifting/stowage/lashing-securing & dunnage, if any special/additional requirement from Marine Insurance surveyor (appointed by shipper/consignee) will be always count additional and extra cost to booking party/shipper/consignee.   
15.	Our BBK rate is based on tackle/tackle basis. Any pre or post tackle charges to be in shipper’s account/Consignee’s account/booking party account.
16.	Direct discharge on consignee vehicle for bbk pkg OR discharge on terminal mafi strictly as per terminal requirement. In case direct discharge on consignee vehicle and If consignee/receiver fails to place the vehicle on time & if vessel will be on hold, then vessel detention/penalty applicable on shipper/consignee/booking party account. Any indirect delivery charges will be on shipper/consignee/booking party account.  
17.	Waiting times of trailer/barges or terminal storage, if incurred, remain full responsibility of the booking party/shipper/consignee. Under any circumstances carrier will not compensate costs related to trailer/barge waiting times or terminal storage. Trailer/Barges are to be available during both loading & discharging port operations upon short notice ordered by the terminal or carriers operations department, to ensure smooth operations both ends.  If trailer/barge is not available at the required time, all related costs and consequences will be for the booking party/shipper/consignee account.
18.	Any quotation obtained on the basis of incomplete or inaccurate information as to the nature or value of the goods shall not be binding for MSC and/or will be considered a material breach of contract, entitling MSC to suspend, modify or cancel the transportation at Merchant’s/shipper’s/consignee’s/booking party’s sole risk and expenses.
19.	Mundra port (CT3 terminal) shipment always subject to Storage ($60/- per day per trailer + GST), Royalty charges (INR 98/- per FRT + GST) on shipper/consignee/booking party a/c. Mundra Port (T3 terminal) shipment always subject to Terminal THC, Royalty on shipper/booking party/consignee a/c. Concern booking party need to directly deal with T3 Terminal. Lifitng plan Must be signed by respective booking party/shipper/consignee.  
20.	Hazira port shipment always subject to Storage, Terminal Royalty Charges, Wharfage, Gujarat Maritime Board Waterfront charges on shipper/consignee/booking party a/c. Concern booking party need to directly deal with Terminal. Lifitng plan Must be signed by respective booking party/shipper/consignee.
21.	Project cargo handled via NSA port cargo always subject to Toll Charges (INR 22/- Per FRT + GST)
22.	Antwerp port quotation always excludes reception/heavy lift charges and always on shipper/consignee/booking party account.

-Any lifting gear used should have CE Marking and CE declaration of Conformity.

       -Shipper’s own lifting gear should, on top of the above, have an inspection-certificate of max 3m old (counting back from day of use in Belgium), issued in  

          Dutch, French, German or English,

         -The inspection certificate must be issued by one of the approved bodies to be found on the following list:

         (https://webgate.ec.europa.eu/single-market-compliance-space/notified-bodies/notified-body-list?filter=countryId:124,notificationStatusId:1)

         -Note that the inspection body must be the exact entity that is on the list

         -In emergencies, where the inspection-certificate is missing or expired upon lifting date in Antwerp, we can instruct one of the local certifying bodies to do the certification prior to              

         handling, (only CE conform gear/tools can be recertified) Costs for re-certification is 1000€, which should be added on top of the handling cost in Antwerp via M/C.

23.	Country specific Requirements:

	1.	Australia: Cargo lifting gears accompanying the cargo and cargo lifting points to be as per the regulations of Australian Govt. Below link for reference Understanding AS4991 - Lifting Devices - LiftQuip Australia <https://www.liftquipaustralia.com.au/understanding-as4991-lifting-devices/> . Necessary lifting lugs force calculation from authorised/competent qualified person from shipper/relevant authority need to be submit to us for obtaining prior acceptance of all shipment passing through Australian port.
	2.	Belgium/EU: Lifting gears need valid CE certificate, and a valid inspection certificate of no older than 3 months before handling in Antwerp. Inspection Certificate can be re issued if required; but valid CE certification is must.

24.	For all the Shanghai export shipments, client will take responsibility along with cost to pick up the empty FR and deliver to terminal. This will not part of our offer.
25.	Any bbk cargo should have suitable lifting points and lashing points to cargo in order to meet vessel requirements. We expect the Booking party/shipper/consignee to strictly follow this requirement and furnish bbk cargo accordingly. If cargo owner fail to meet this requirement then carrier reserve all rights to reject your cargo. All cancellation and shut out charges to apply to Booking party/shipper/consignee.
26.	Subject to applicable GST on BBK terminal handling charges. GST will be as per Indian Govt. Rule. GST on freight at actual as per govt regulation.
27.	Subject to separate BL/Inv/Pkg List for BBK cargo and separate BL/Inv/Pkg List for Unitized/FCL/OOG cargo. If combined BL required, it will be subject to approval.
28.	For Any BBK cargo our offer ALWAYS EXCLUDES custom clearance at POL and at POD.  
29.	Subject to THC and Locals charges for containerized/unitized/FCL/OOG cargo. For OOG dimensions/wt. if any time base loading/discharge charges by terminal same will be on shipper/consignee/booking party a/c.
30.	Unitized container rate is basis on container plan proposed by shipper/Nominated forwarder/CHA. Any increase or decrease in container volume/dimensions/weight; rate will change.
31.	Subject to applicable GST on local charges for containerized/unitized/FCL/OOG cargo. GST will be as per Indian Govt. Rule. GST on freight at actual as per govt regulation.
32.	For containerized/unitized/FCL/OOG cargo our offer ALWAYS EXCLUDES empty pickup, stuffing, custom clearance, loaded containers port movement at POL and wise versa at POD.  
33.	Rates valid for declared dimensions & weight at time of quotation only. If any change in dimensions/weight our rates will stand null and void.
34.	https://www.msc.com/en/local-information/asia-pacific/india#LocalRequirements Indian ports Local charges are available at given link.
35.	Rate Validity End May 2025
